title: hypericum hirtellum var. hirtellum
intro: Hypericum hirtellum var. hirtellum, also known as small St. John's Wort, is a shrubby plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Hypericum hirtellum var. hirtellum include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it useful for treating skin conditions and minor infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with other medications.
